content:
💡 ghgrab | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To efficiently download specific assets from GitHub repositories directly from the command line, avoiding unnecessary cloning and streamlining workflows.
✨ Key Features: • Search and download files/folders from GitHub via CLI • TUI (Terminal User Interface) with fuzzy search • File preview within the TUI • Support for GitHub LFS • Batch download mode
📖 Summary: ghgrab is a Rust-based command-line tool that provides a user-friendly terminal interface for searching and downloading specific files or entire folders from GitHub repositories without needing to perform a full `git clone`. It offers features like fuzzy search, file preview, batch downloads, and an agent mode for scripting.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 folderhost | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a simple, self-hosted cloud storage and collaboration solution that is easy to deploy and manage, especially for users seeking a lightweight alternative to more complex platforms.
✨ Key Features: • Single Binary Deployment • No Dependencies Required • High Performance (Go backend + React frontend) • Real-time Collaboration (Monaco Editor) • Multi-user Support with Permissions
📖 Summary: FolderHost is a self-hosted cloud platform distributed as a single, dependency-free executable. It allows users to share files, collaborate in real-time with live code editing, and manage users, offering a lightweight alternative to complex solutions like Nextcloud.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 sentrysearch | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Finding specific moments or events within video footage using natural language queries.
✨ Key Features: • Semantic search over video files (mp4) • Uses Gemini Embedding 2 or Qwen3-VL for embeddings • Stores embeddings in ChromaDB • Splits videos into chunks for indexing • Returns trimmed video clips as search results
📖 Summary: SentrySearch enables semantic search over video footage. It allows users to find specific moments within videos by typing descriptive text queries, and the system returns a trimmed clip of the relevant segment.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 awesome-seo-tools | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: Discovering and selecting SEO tools to enhance website performance and search engine rankings.
✨ Key Features: • Curated list of SEO tools • Categorized tools for specific SEO needs • Includes tools for keyword research, backlink analysis, content optimization, rank tracking, technical SEO, local SEO, browser extensions, and more • Open for contributions to expand the list
📖 Summary: This repository is a curated list of Search Engine Optimization (SEO) tools designed to help users improve their website's ranking and visibility on search engines. It categorizes various tools for different SEO needs, from keyword research to technical analysis.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ winscript | CSS
🎯 Primary Use Case: Customizing and optimizing a Windows installation for improved privacy, performance, and a cleaner user experience.
✨ Key Features: • Debloat pre-installed applications and Windows features • Enhance privacy by disabling telemetry and data collection • Optimize system performance through various tweaks • Bulk install applications using Chocolatey or Winget
📖 Summary: WinScript is an open-source Windows customization tool that allows users to debloat unnecessary applications, enhance privacy by disabling data collection, optimize performance by fine-tuning system settings, and easily install desired software using package managers like Chocolatey or Winget.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
